core_content.markdown:
## What is a digital image?

Mathematical definition:

A 2D (grayscale)digital image is a 2D function defined on a 2D domain (usually rectangular domain):

$$f:\Omega\to\mathbb{R}$$

$f(x,y)$ is called the brightness/intensity/grey level;

$(\mathbf{x},\mathbf{y})$ is the spatial coordinates of the image.

Thus,a 2D digital image looks like this:

$$f(x,y)=\left[\begin{matrix}{f(1,1)}&{f(1,2)}&{\dots}&{f(1,N)}\\ {f(2,1)}&{f(2,2)}&{\dots}&{f(2,N)}\\ {\vdots}&{\vdots}&{}&{\vdots}\\ {f(N,1)}&{f(N,2)}&{\dots}&{f(N,N)}\\ \end{matrix}\right]$$

Each element in the matrix is called pixel (picture element);

Usually,$0\leq f(x,y)\leq G-1\quad{\mathrm{\boldmath~{\sf~a n d}~}}\left(N=2^{n},G=2^{m}\right)$ 

IMAGE PROCESSING IS RELATED TOLINEAR ALGEBRA!!

rec_texts: image is a 2D function defined on a 2D domain
